tldr: Move from Courier's developer-first multi-channel notification API to Airship's mature mobile engagement platform with native iOS/Android/Web SDKs.
why_switch: Need first-party mobile SDKs (iOS, Android, Web) rather than an API-first abstraction
Want a more enterprise-grade audience and segmentation model
Prefer a long-standing vendor with established mobile push expertise
Looking for richer in-app messaging UI and journey tooling on mobile
Consolidate channels under one provider with stronger SLAs
what_to_export: Notification templates (per channel: email, push, SMS, in-app)
User profiles with identifiers and channel tokens
Routing rules and channel priority logic
Tags, properties, and custom user attributes
Brand assets (logos, colors) used in templates
Webhook and integration credentials (regenerate at target)
Historical send logs (for reference, if needed)
export_method: Use the Courier API to list and export users, profiles, lists, templates (notifications), and brands as JSON. There is no single one-click bulk export, so script the dump per resource and store backups locally. Consult Courier's official API reference for the exact endpoints and pagination before scripting.
import_method: Recreate templates in Airship's Message Center / template builder (or via Airship's REST API if available for your plan), then re-register devices through the Airship SDKs in your apps. Migrate user attributes via Airship's Named User and Custom Events APIs. Validate via test sends to a staging audience before pointing production traffic. Verify the exact endpoints in Airship's official docs.

concept_mapping.note: Airship separates the persistent user (Named User) from device channels
Template syntax differs; rebuild rather than auto-translate
Multi-channel fallback logic must be reimplemented in Airship's flow tools
Map 1:1 where possible; long-form data goes to Attributes
Recreate visual styles inside Airship templates
Static lists vs dynamic segments — choose per use case

gotchas: Mobile push tokens must be re-registered via Airship SDK; you cannot transfer APNs/FCM tokens 1:1
User identity model differs (Named User vs Courier user_id) — plan an alias strategy
Template syntax is not portable; expect to rebuild content per channel
Preference center / subscription semantics differ; double-check unsubscribe handling for compliance
Pricing model jumps from freemium API to paid subscription — re-estimate budget early
Run dual-send during cutover to verify deliverability before turning Courier off

email.subject: Migrating from Courier to Airship
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to migrate our notifications stack from Courier to Airship. The goal is to lean on Airship's first-party mobile SDKs and mature audience tooling.

High-level plan:
- Export templates, users, preferences, and routing config from Courier via its API
- Rebuild templates and journeys in Airship
- Integrate Airship SDKs in iOS/Android/Web and re-register channels
- Run dual-send for a short window to validate deliverability
- Cut over and decommission Courier

Estimated effort is 20-60 hours depending on template count and journey complexity. I'll share a detailed checklist next.

Thanks
